ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus Group DLL side-loading via trojanized signed binary (Operation DreamJob / ZetaNile / 3CX)
ucs.description: Hunts the Lazarus signature install pattern: a legitimate signed third-party binary (PuTTY/AmazonVNC/FreeCAD/3CXDesktopApp/TightVNC/Comparator) executes from a user-writable / removable / ISO-mount path and loads an unsigned sibling DLL from the same folder. Lazarus has used this exact pair-shipping technique across DreamJob LinkedIn lures (ZetaNile, LPEClient), the AppleJeus crypto app campaigns, and the March 2023 3CX supply-chain compromise (ffmpeg.dll side-loaded by 3CXDesktopApp.exe).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("PuTTY.exe","amazon_ssh_client_for_windows.exe","vncviewer.exe","AmazonVNC.exe","Comparator.exe","FreeCAD.exe","3CXDesktopApp.exe","WinSCP.exe","TightVNC.exe","ffmpeg.exe") (Processes.process_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Desktop\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="D:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="E:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="F:\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created Filesystem.file_name IN ("colorui.dll","vncutil.dll","ffmpeg.dll","msvcr100.dll","libcurl.dll","wsock32.dll","version.dll","mscoree.dll","d3d9.dll","dbghelp.dll","VERSION.dll","d3d11.dll") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_name as sideloaded_dll file_path as dll_path] | where parent!="msiexec.exe" AND parent!="explorer.exe" | table firstTime dest user process_name process_path sideloaded_dll dll_path parent cmdline
ucs.kql:
let _lazarus_carrier_bins = dynamic(["PuTTY.exe","amazon_ssh_client_for_windows.exe","vncviewer.exe","AmazonVNC.exe","Comparator.exe","FreeCAD.exe","3CXDesktopApp.exe","WinSCP.exe","TightVNC.exe","ffmpeg.exe"]);
let _known_sideload_dlls = dynamic(["colorui.dll","vncutil.dll","ffmpeg.dll","msvcr100.dll","libcurl.dll","wsock32.dll","version.dll","mscoree.dll","d3d9.dll","dbghelp.dll","d3d11.dll"]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\Downloads\", @"\Desktop\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_lazarus_carrier_bins)
    or (FileName in~ (_known_sideload_dlls) and FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_user_writable)
    or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath              // sibling DLL
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256                       // not the EXE itself
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
   and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          CarrierBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          CarrierPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          CarrierVendor = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
          CarrierSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideloadedDLL = FileName,
          DLLPath = FolderPath,
          DLLSHA256 = SHA256,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _lazarus_carrier_bins = dynamic(["PuTTY.exe","amazon_ssh_client_for_windows.exe","vncviewer.exe","AmazonVNC.exe","Comparator.exe","FreeCAD.exe","3CXDesktopApp.exe","WinSCP.exe","TightVNC.exe","ffmpeg.exe"]);
let _known_sideload_dlls = dynamic(["colorui.dll","vncutil.dll","ffmpeg.dll","msvcr100.dll","libcurl.dll","wsock32.dll","version.dll","mscoree.dll","d3d9.dll","dbghelp.dll","d3d11.dll"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7    // ImageLoaded
| extend Image          = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded    = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         Signed         = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         User           = tostring(EventData.User),
         OrigFileName   = tostring(EventData.OriginalFileName),
         ImageHash      = tostring(EventData.Hashes),
         LoadedHash     = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| extend ImageBin       = tostring(split(Image, @"\")[-1]),
         ImageDir       = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\"),
         LoadedBin      = tostring(split(ImageLoaded, @"\")[-1]),
         LoadedDir      = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")
| where ImageBin in~ (_lazarus_carrier_bins)
    or LoadedBin in~ (_known_sideload_dlls)
| where ImageDir =~ LoadedDir
| where ImageDir has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\Desktop\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Public\")
    or ImageDir matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
| where not(ImageDir startswith @"C:\Windows\")
   and not(ImageDir startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where User !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User,
          CarrierBin = ImageBin, CarrierPath = ImageDir,
          SideloadedDLL = LoadedBin, DLLPath = LoadedDir,
          Signed, OrigFileName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus Group DLL Side-Loading via Trojanized Signed Binary
id: 7e1a5d1a-6e47-4f1a-9f3d-1f3d2c4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects Lazarus Group (Diamond Sleet / ZINC / Hidden Cobra) DLL side-loading where a legitimate signed third-party application executes from a user-writable or ISO-mount location and loads a sibling DLL with a name historically abused by Lazarus (ZetaNile / DreamJob / 3CX supply-chain).
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/09/29/zinc-weaponizing-open-source-software/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/3cx-software-supply-chain-compromise
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1027.009
  - attack.g0032
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  carrier_bin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\PuTTY.exe'
      - '\amazon_ssh_client_for_windows.exe'
      - '\vncviewer.exe'
      - '\AmazonVNC.exe'
      - '\Comparator.exe'
      - '\FreeCAD.exe'
      - '\3CXDesktopApp.exe'
      - '\WinSCP.exe'
      - '\TightVNC.exe'
      - '\ffmpeg.exe'
  sideloaded_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\colorui.dll'
      - '\vncutil.dll'
      - '\ffmpeg.dll'
      - '\msvcr100.dll'
      - '\libcurl.dll'
      - '\wsock32.dll'
      - '\version.dll'
      - '\mscoree.dll'
      - '\d3d9.dll'
      - '\d3d11.dll'
  user_writable_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\Desktop\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - 'D:\'
      - 'E:\'
      - 'F:\'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: (carrier_bin or sideloaded_dll) and user_writable_path and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Portable apps run from removable media by power users (rare in enterprise estates)
  - Developer machines testing the actual signed application from a build folder
level: high
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading via a trojanized but legitimately-signed third-party binary is the single most repeated install-stage technique across Lazarus campaigns: Operation DreamJob 2022 (ZetaNile) used trojanized PuTTY + colorui.dll and AmazonVNC + vncutil.dll against aerospace/defense recruiters; AppleJeus paired Comparator.exe with malicious sideload DLLs; the March 2023 3CX supply-chain compromise (UN

_field_issues.message: `InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName` is not a column of DeviceImageLoadEvents; it does exist on DeviceProcessEvents, DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceFileEvents.

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus Group ISO/IMG-mount LOLBin internet egress (DreamJob initial-access chain)
ucs.description: Catches the Lazarus DreamJob delivery chain: a LinkedIn-lure ISO/IMG/VHD is mounted as a removable volume, the user double-clicks an embedded LNK, and a Windows LOLBin (mshta, regsvr32, rundll32, wscript, cscript, curl) executes from the mount drive (D:\, E:\) and immediately calls out to attacker infrastructure (often a compromised WordPress site or HostingerVPS C2). The combination of mount-drive parent path + LOLBin child + outbound HTTP within 60s is a very narrow, highly Lazarus-correlated signal.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") (Processes.process_path="D:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="E:\\*" OR Processes.process_path="F:\\*" OR Processes.parent_process="*.lnk*" OR Processes.parent_process="*.iso*" OR Processes.parent_process="*.img*" OR Processes.parent_process="*.vhd*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename _time as proc_time | join type=inner dest pid [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as remote_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as remote_port values(All_Traffic.app) as remote_url from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443,8080,8443) NOT (All_Traffic.dest_category="internal" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="10.0.0.0/8" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="172.16.0.0/12" OR All_Traffic.dest_ip="192.168.0.0/16") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename _time as net_time process_id as pid src as dest] | where net_time >= proc_time AND net_time <= (proc_time + 60) | table firstTime dest user process_name process_path parent cmdline remote_ip remote_port remote_url
ucs.kql:
let _lookback = 7d;
let _window_sec = 60;
let _lolbins = dynamic(["mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let _proc = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where FileName in~ (_lolbins)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
        or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
        or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".lnk", ".iso", ".img", ".vhd")
        or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
           and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\"
    | project ProcStart = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              LolBin = FileName,
              LolBinPath = FolderPath,
              LolBinPid = ProcessId,
              ProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
_proc
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemotePort in (80, 443, 8080, 8443)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_lolbins)
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, NetPid = InitiatingProcessId,
              RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl
  ) on DeviceId
| where NetPid == LolBinPid
| where NetTime between (ProcStart .. ProcStart + _window_sec * 1s)
| project ProcStart, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, ProcStart),
          DeviceName, AccountName, LolBin, LolBinPath,
          ProcessCommandLine, Parent, ParentCmd,
          RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl
| order by ProcStart desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _lookback = 7d;
let _window_sec = 60;
let _lolbins = dynamic(["mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let _proc = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where ChildBin in~ (_lolbins)
    | where TargetProcessName matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
        or ParentProcessCommandLine has_any (".lnk", ".iso", ".img", ".vhd")
        or ParentProcessName endswith "explorer.exe"
           and TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\"
    | project ProcStart = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              LolBin = ChildBin, LolBinPath = TargetProcessName,
              LolBinPid = TargetProcessId,
              TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessName,
              ParentProcessCommandLine;
_proc
| join kind=inner (
    ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where DstPortNumber in (80, 443, 8080, 8443)
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
    | extend NetBin = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where NetBin in~ (_lolbins)
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              NetPid = ActingProcessId,
              DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url
  ) on DvcHostname
| where NetPid == LolBinPid
| where NetTime between (ProcStart .. ProcStart + _window_sec * 1s)
| project ProcStart, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, ProcStart),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername, LolBin, LolBinPath,
          TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
          DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url
| order by ProcStart desc
ucs.rationale: Lazarus / Labyrinth Chollima's DreamJob playbook against defense, aerospace, and crypto employees consistently delivers job-description payloads inside ISO/IMG/VHD containers — the user mounts the file as drive D:\/E:\ and clicks an embedded LNK that proxies to mshta or regsvr32 fetching a Manuscrypt/BLINDINGCAN HTA from compromised WordPress sites (Microsoft, Mandiant, ESET, and Kaspersky have al
